name: Ashen — DM Codex: Bestiary & Boss Cards

pages.name: How to Read a Moveset Card

text.content:
Every enemy here has one or more Moveset Cards. A card is everything you need to run that attack at the table.

Card anatomy

  • Telegraph (W1 / W2 / W3) — the three beats you narrate out loud: wind-up, commit, impact.
  • Target & damage — who it hits and for how much (on a failed defense).
  • Defensive Profile — the 3x3 grid of how each defense fares in each window: ADV (advantage), — (normal), DIS (disadvantage), or X (impossible). This is the hidden puzzle; do not show players.
  • Tags — impossibility flags (unblockable / undodgeable / unparryable) and special notes.
  • Payoff — what a clean parry or a behind-the-enemy dodge sets up.

Defensive DCs by tier

If a card does not list a custom DC, use these flat-d20 defaults (no ability mod is added — quality comes from the window read):

MookDodge DC 10 · Parry DC 12
Mini-bossDodge DC 12 · Parry DC 14
Major bossDodge DC 13 · Parry DC 15

Remember: a correct read makes them roll that DC with advantage; a wrong read, disadvantage. Block uses no roll: a shield subtracts a flat % per damage type (see its gear card), heavy moves only chip through, and a block never staggers (that is parry’s job). Block ignores the window, so a move’s three Block cells always match. On the Block row: X = unblockable; chip = heavy (blockable, only chips); half = a lesser shield halves it; — = an ordinary block.

Reveal discipline

Players earn the grid by playing. Mooks reveal in 1–2 exchanges naturally. Bosses reveal through the Tell Ladder on each wipe. Never read the grid aloud — narrate the fiction and let them deduce it.

pages.name: Running Bosses — HP, Cadence & Table Talk

text.content:
Three knobs keep every boss fair and on-clock. Set HP before the session, then watch the round-1 audit.

Boss HP (set before play)

HP is not an attrition bar — a correct defensive read negates the hit, so HP measures how many rounds of good play the party must string together. The numbers on each stat block assume a 4-player party:

Iudex Gundyr (P1 / Pus P2)75 / 65
Outrider Knight (mini)190
Pus of Man (High Wall mini)190
The Dragon (secret)200 (drive-off at ~50 in the dead zone)
Vordt (climax)625 (Phase 2 at ~312)

Headcount scaling: multiply the listed HP by (players ÷ 4) and round to the nearest 5 — a 3-player Vordt ≈ 470, a 6-player Vordt ≈ 940. (The Level-Up / Assemble flow does this for you, but verify.)

The round-1 audit (the only live adjustment)

After the first full round, total the damage the party actually landed and compare it to listed HP ÷ target rounds (Gundyr ~3 rounds, minis ~4, a Dragon kill ~5–6, Vordt ~6). If they are hitting much harder or softer than expected, nudge HP by ±10–15% so the fight lands in its target length. Do it once, then stop fiddling.

Boss attack cadence (so the fight has teeth)

A boss should threaten roughly 2–3 attacks per round — one on its turn plus 1–2 legendary actions on the party’s turns — for about 15–20 telegraphed attacks across a 6–8 round fight. Spread them across the party; do not focus-fire one PC to death. Legendary actions are your pacing valve: spend them to keep pressure between turns, hold them when someone is on the ropes.

Table talk is encouraged

The targeted player always declares their own defense and window — it is their read to make. But the whole table may freely advise, point out tells, and call out what worked last time. Souls is a co-op puzzle and the chatter is the fun; just keep the final call with the player in the hot seat.

pages.name: Boss Quick-Grids — Every Moveset at a Glance

text.content:
Every boss moveset on one screen — the grids only, for fast mid-fight lookup. Each cell is the result for that defense × window: ADV / normal / DIS / X (impossible). Block uses the deterministic legend (— ordinary · half · chip · X unblockable). For telegraphs, payoffs, damage, and Tell Ladders, open the boss's full card (the pages that follow).

Iudex Gundyr — Phase 1

AC 16 · HP 75 (×players÷4) · 2 legendary actions · Trigger: at 0 HP he does NOT die — the Pus of Man erupts (Phase 2).

1 · Overhead Chop — fully parryable (clean swing)

W1W2W3
Dodge—ADVDIS
Block———
ParryDISDISADV

2 · Wide Sweep — unparryable (too wide)

W1W2W3
Dodge—ADV—
Block———
ParryXXX

3 · Thrust Lunge — parryable (a clean thrust — parry on the commit)

W1W2W3
Dodge—ADV (sidestep)—
Block———
ParryDISADVDIS

4 · Shield Kick — unparryable & unblockable — it punishes a raised shield; don’t wall it, sidestep (dodge W2). Low damage — a tempo/positioning move

W1W2W3
Dodge—ADV—
BlockXXX
ParryXXX

Pus of Man — Gundyr Eruption (Phase 2)

AC 15 · HP 65 · reach 15 ft · Vulnerable: fire. Souls roll into Gundyr's 2,500.

1 · Tendril Multi-lash — unparryable

W1W2W3
Dodge—ADV—
Blockhalfhalfhalf
ParryXXX

2 · Grab — unblockable, unparryable; must be dodged EARLY (read W1). The lesson in reading a slow tell

W1W2W3
DodgeADVDISX
BlockXXX
ParryXXX

3 · Ground Slam (cluster punisher) — unparryable; punishes clustering — dodge must start on the rear-up (W1) to clear the radius

W1W2W3
DodgeADV (out of burst)DIS—
Blockhalfhalfhalf
ParryXXX

Outrider Knight (mini-boss)

AC 17 · HP 190 · Speed 40 · Anti-turtle: if no PC damaged it last round, it gains an extra action this round. Frost claws build Frostbite.

1 · Triple Claw Combo — parryable only on the third claw (W3) — a hard, rewarding read. Blocking only chips it down; you must answer aggressively

W1W2W3
DodgeDISADV—
Blockchipchipchip
ParryDISDISADV

2 · Frost Lunge — undodgeable head-on (it tracks) — you must sidestep the LINE on W1. Unblockable (a charge) and unparryable

W1W2W3
DodgeADV (sidestep the line)X—
BlockXXX
ParryXXX

3 · Pounce (turtle punish) — unparryable; the more they turtle, the more this comes out

W1W2W3
Dodge—ADVDIS
BlockXXX
ParryXXX

Pus of Man — High Wall (mini-boss)

AC 14 · HP 190 · reach 15 ft · Vulnerable: fire (double). Ambush from a 'dead' hollow.

1 · Whipping Tendrils — unparryable

W1W2W3
Dodge—ADV—
Blockhalfhalfhalf
ParryXXX

2 · Lunge Bite — parryable on the commit (W2); a clean riposte staggers it for a big fire follow-up

W1W2W3
Dodge—ADV—
Block———
ParryDISADVDIS

3 · Black Spew (ranged) — unparryable; dodge early to leave the cone

W1W2W3
DodgeADV (clear the cone)DIS—
Blockhalfhalfhalf
ParryXXX

The Dragon (secret boss)

AC 18 · HP 200 · Fly 80 · Cover gauntlet: no damage race in the open — Dash cover-to-cover into the dead zone. 3 paths (bypass / drive off / hunt).

1 · Fire Breath (bridge rake) — unblockable, unparryable, undodgeable in the open. The ONLY answer is cover — survive it by position, not by roll. In the dead zone it cannot reach you

W1W2W3
Dodgereach cover (gauntlet roll)X—
BlockXXX
ParryXXX

2 · Tail Sweep (dead-zone punisher) — unparryable; the price of admission to the soft belly — watch for it

W1W2W3
Dodge—ADVDIS
Blockchipchipchip
ParryXXX

3 · Claw & Bite (dead-zone) — the bite (W3) is parryable; the claw is not. A parry here is huge — it staggers a dragon

W1W2W3
Dodge—ADV—
Block———
ParryXDISADV (bite only)

Vordt of the Boreal Valley (final boss)

AC 18 · HP 625 (Phase 2 at ~50% / 312) · Vulnerable: fire & lightning. Builds Frostbite. Phase 2 = frenzy + cold aura + the two Phase-2 moves below.

1 · Overhead Mace Smash — parryable on impact (W3); blockable, but a heavy mace only chips through (no stagger)

W1W2W3
Dodge—ADVDIS
Blockchipchipchip
ParryDISDISADV

2 · Wide Sweep — unparryable; dodge under the swing on W2

W1W2W3
Dodge—ADV—
Blockhalfhalfhalf
ParryXXX

3 · Frost Charge — undodgeable head-on (it tracks the lane) — sidestep OUT of the lane on W1. Unblockable (a charge) and unparryable

W1W2W3
DodgeADV (sidestep the lane)X—
BlockXXX
ParryXXX

4 · Frostbreath Cone (Phase 2) — unblockable, unparryable; dodge early to leave the cone

W1W2W3
DodgeADV (clear the cone)DIS—
BlockXXX
ParryXXX

5 · Grab-Leap (Phase 2) — unblockable, unparryable; dodge on the read (W1)

W1W2W3
DodgeADVDISX
BlockXXX
ParryXXX

Role: first major boss; teaches the whole defensive system with clean, readable swings. Be generous — this fight should feel winnable on the second or third try.

Stat block — Phase 1 (Champion Gundyr)

AC 16 · HP 75 (4-player baseline) · Speed 30 ft · Souls 2,500 (paid out after Phase 2). Halberd strikes: 2d10+4 slashing. Saves favor Str/Con. 2 legendary actions (halberd jab 1d10+4; 5-ft reposition).

Phase trigger: at 0 HP in Phase 1 he does NOT die — the Pus of Man erupts from his back (Phase 2). This is Teaching Death #1: the relieved, low-Estus party gets mauled. Telegraph it: "as he falls, the wound on his back splits open and something black uncoils…"

Moveset — Phase 1

1 · Overhead Chop (the teaching move). Telegraph: W1 he hauls the halberd high overhead · W2 it arcs down · W3 it craters where you stand. Target: one PC, 2d10+4.

W1W2W3
Dodge—ADVDIS
Block———
ParryDISDISADV

Tags: fully parryable (clean swing). Payoff: the move that teaches parry — reward a W3 parry with a big party riposte and an audible stagger.

2 · Wide Sweep. Telegraph: W1 winds the halberd back across his body · W2 horizontal sweep (all PCs in a 10-ft arc) · W3 over-rotation leaves him open. Target: arc, 2d10+4.

W1W2W3
Dodge—ADV—
Block———
ParryXXX

Tags: unparryable (too wide). Payoff: after W3 he is briefly open — a dodge into his flank sets up advantage.

3 · Thrust Lunge. Telegraph: W1 coils low · W2 lunges the point forward (reach 15 ft) · W3 retracts. Target: one PC in a line, 2d10+4.

W1W2W3
Dodge—ADV (sidestep)—
Block———
ParryDISADVDIS

Tags: parryable (a clean thrust — parry on the commit). Payoff: sidestep dodge ends behind him.

4 · Shield Kick. Telegraph: W1 a small weight-shift · W2 a stomping kick · W3 recover. Target: one PC, 1d8+4 and pushed 5 ft (stagger if Poise < 3).

W1W2W3
Dodge—ADV—
BlockXXX
ParryXXX

Tags: unparryable & unblockable — it punishes a raised shield; don’t wall it, sidestep (dodge W2). Low damage — a tempo/positioning move.

Tell Ladder — Gundyr

  1. Wipe 1: "His overhead is slow and honest — there's a heartbeat where the blade hangs before it falls." (Points at parrying the Overhead on impact.)
  2. Wipe 2: "The wide sweep can't be turned aside, only slipped under — wait for the swing, then move." (Dodge the Sweep on W2.)
  3. Wipe 3: "His shield-bash and kick can't be turned aside OR walled — raising your guard does nothing against them. Read the tell and step out of the way." (Teaches the unblockable read: dodge it, don't block it.)
  4. Wipe 4: "When he 'dies,' he doesn't. Keep a flask and a held action — something comes out of him." (Prepares them for Phase 2.)

pages.name: BOSS — Gundyr Phase 2 / Pus of Man (eruption)

text.content:
The black tendril-mass that bursts from Champion Gundyr. Continues the same fight at full menace against a party that thought they had won.

Stat block

AC 15 · HP 65 (4-player baseline) · Speed 30 ft, reach 15 ft · Vulnerable: fire. Souls roll into Gundyr's 2,500 total. The host body still moves it — long reach, grabs, ground slams.

Moveset

1 · Tendril Multi-lash. W1 the tendrils rear and fan out · W2 they whip across the front (up to 3 PCs in reach) · W3 recoil. Target: up to 3, 2d8+4 each.

W1W2W3
Dodge—ADV—
Blockhalfhalfhalf
ParryXXX

Tags: unparryable.

2 · Grab. W1 a single tendril snakes toward one PC (clear tell) · W2 it snatches · W3 reels them in. Target: one PC, 1d10 + grappled (2d6 crush/round until escape, Athletics/Acrobatics DC 14).

W1W2W3
DodgeADVDISX
BlockXXX
ParryXXX

Tags: unblockable, unparryable; must be dodged EARLY (read W1). The lesson in reading a slow tell.

3 · Ground Slam (cluster punisher). W1 the whole mass rears up high · W2 it crashes down (burst, all within 10 ft) · W3 settles. Target: 10-ft burst, 3d8 + knocked prone.

W1W2W3
DodgeADV (out of burst)DIS—
Blockhalfhalfhalf
ParryXXX

Tags: unparryable; punishes clustering — dodge must start on the rear-up (W1) to clear the radius.

Running Teaching Death #1

The party will likely be low on Estus and clustered for the "victory." Let the eruption land hard. Lesson at the bonfire (Gundyr Ladder rung 3): never spend your last flask on a boss that isn't fully dead; hold resources and spread out. Fire damage melts it — if a pyromancer figures that out, reward it.

Role: a fast, relentless duel that punishes passivity. Teaching Death #3: a shield-camping party gets ground to dust. The lesson is aggression and footwork.

Stat block

AC 17 · HP 190 (4-player baseline) · Speed 40 ft · Souls 1,000. Frost claws build Frostbite. Anti-turtle: if no PC dealt it damage on the previous round, it gains an extra action this round (it "hastes" / frenzies from being ignored).

Moveset

1 · Triple Claw Combo. Three fast swipes — the puzzle is which beat is the opening. W1 first claw · W2 second claw · W3 third claw (over-commit). Target: one PC, 3 x 1d8+3 (+Frostbite each hit that lands).

W1W2W3
DodgeDISADV—
Blockchipchipchip
ParryDISDISADV

Tags: parryable only on the third claw (W3) — a hard, rewarding read. Blocking only chips it down; you must answer aggressively.

2 · Frost Lunge. A 40-ft gap-closer. W1 it drops into a sprinter's coil · W2 it rockets across the gap · W3 slashing arrival. Target: one PC in the line, 2d8+3 + Frostbite.

W1W2W3
DodgeADV (sidestep the line)X—
BlockXXX
ParryXXX

Tags: undodgeable head-on (it tracks) — you must sidestep the LINE on W1. Unblockable (a charge) and unparryable.

3 · Pounce (turtle punish). Only used when the party went passive (its anti-turtle trigger). W1 it crouches and coils · W2 it leaps onto the most defensive PC · W3 pins and rakes. Target: one PC, 2d10+3 + knocked prone.

W1W2W3
Dodge—ADVDIS
BlockXXX
ParryXXX

Tags: unparryable; the more they turtle, the more this comes out.

Tell Ladder — Outrider

  1. Wipe 1: "It never stops moving. Standing still and blocking just feeds it — it hits harder the longer you wait." (Reveals the anti-turtle mechanic.)
  2. Wipe 2: "The triple-swipe over-commits on the last claw; that's the only beat you can turn aside." (Parry the combo on W3.)
  3. Wipe 3: "Its charge follows you — don't backpedal, cut sideways out of the lane as it sets its feet." (Sidestep the Frost Lunge on W1.)

pages.name: MINI-BOSS — Pus of Man (High Wall, fire-weak)

text.content:
Role: a "dead" hollow on the wall bursts into an abomination as the party passes. A shock ambush that rewards anyone carrying fire.

Stat block

AC 14 · HP 190 (4-player baseline) · Speed 30 ft, reach 15 ft · Vulnerable: fire (takes double) · Souls 1,000. Telegraph the ambush: a slumped hollow they could have walked past suddenly convulses.

Moveset

1 · Whipping Tendrils. W1 tendrils rise and fan · W2 they lash the front line (up to 2 PCs) · W3 recoil. Target: up to 2, 2d6+3 each.

W1W2W3
Dodge—ADV—
Blockhalfhalfhalf
ParryXXX

Tags: unparryable.

2 · Lunge Bite. The host head rears and strikes — a committed, parryable lunge. W1 the head pulls back · W2 it snaps forward · W3 it hangs, jaws wide. Target: one PC, 2d8+3.

W1W2W3
Dodge—ADV—
Block———
ParryDISADVDIS

Tags: parryable on the commit (W2); a clean riposte staggers it for a big fire follow-up.

3 · Black Spew (ranged). W1 the mass swells and gurgles · W2 it vomits a 15-ft cone of corruption · W3 drips. Target: cone, 3d6 poison + reduced healing 1 round.

W1W2W3
DodgeADV (clear the cone)DIS—
Blockhalfhalfhalf
ParryXXX

Tags: unparryable; dodge early to leave the cone.

Tell

It recoils visibly from torch and flame. One rung is enough: "fire makes it scream and shrivel — it fears the flame." A pyromancer or a thrown firebomb roughly halves this fight.

Role: an optional, killable secret boss on the High Wall rampart. Visible the whole time — a fight the party chooses to start. Teaching Death #2 lives here: charging the open bridge = incineration.

Stat block

AC 18 · HP 200 (4-player baseline) · Fly 80 ft · Souls 3,000–4,000 + a unique drop (titanite/twinkling or a unique Ember) — path C only. Perches on the far tower and rakes the bridge with fire on a rhythm.

The terrain is the solution

The rampart is lined with alcoves, pillars, and doorways. Charred corpses cluster in the open — the tell that the open bridge is a kill-zone. A message on the ground reads: "Behind, then attack" / "Cover, don't run."

The gauntlet (how to cross)

The fire breath has gaps. To advance, a PC Dashes cover-to-cover between breaths: when they break cover, they make a flat d20 (Dex or Athletics flavor) vs the breath timer — succeed and they reach the next cover; fail and they are caught in the open for the next breath. Reaching the dead zone beneath/behind the dragon takes them out of the fire and exposes its belly and legs.

Moveset

1 · Fire Breath (bridge rake). The rhythm move. W1 it rears back and inhales (huge tell) · W2 it sweeps flame across the whole open bridge · W3 lingering flame pools. Target: everyone in the open, 4d8 fire.

W1W2W3
Dodgereach cover (gauntlet roll)X—
BlockXXX
ParryXXX

Tags: unblockable, unparryable, undodgeable in the open. The ONLY answer is cover — survive it by position, not by roll. In the dead zone it cannot reach you.

2 · Tail Sweep (dead-zone punisher). When PCs are beneath/behind it. W1 the tail coils across the stone · W2 it sweeps in a 15-ft arc · W3 settles. Target: arc, 3d10 + knocked prone.

W1W2W3
Dodge—ADVDIS
Blockchipchipchip
ParryXXX

Tags: unparryable; the price of admission to the soft belly — watch for it.

3 · Claw & Bite (dead-zone). W1 it twists its head/foreclaw down · W2 it strikes the cluster beneath · W3 lingers, jaws open. Target: one PC, 3d8+5.

W1W2W3
Dodge—ADV—
Block———
ParryXDISADV (bite only)

Tags: the bite (W3) is parryable; the claw is not. A parry here is huge — it staggers a dragon.

The three resolution paths (player choice)

  1. A · Bypass. Just time the breath and cross using cover. Obstacle cleared, dragon alive, no reward. Always available — they can get past without ever flanking it.
  2. B · Flank to drive off. Reach the dead zone and deal threshold damage (~50 HP). The dragon beats its wings (knockback gust) and flies off to a new perch, still in sight — wounded, retreating. Immediate obstacle cleared; move on.
  3. C · Hunt to kill. After it flees to the visible perch, the party may CHOOSE to chase it — cross to its new position and re-run a shorter gauntlet — to actually kill it. Greedy, dangerous, optional. Pays the full secret-boss reward + unique drop.

Tell Ladder — Dragon

  1. Wipe 1: "The open bridge is a killing field — those corpses didn't make it across. Get off the open stone when it inhales."
  2. Wipe 2: "The fire comes in waves with gaps between. Advance in the lulls, cover to cover."
  3. Wipe 3: "Directly beneath and behind it the flame can't reach, and the belly is soft — but it won't sit still; mind the tail."

pages.name: BOSS — Vordt of the Boreal Valley (the exam)

text.content:
Role: the climax and the final exam. Everything they learned — reading windows, dodging, spreading out, managing a status bar — is tested here. Phase 2 is Teaching Death #4.

Stat block

AC 18 · HP 625 (4-player baseline; Phase 2 at ~312) · Speed 40 ft · Souls 3,000 · Vulnerable: fire and thunder (frost-brittle joints). Great mace: 2d8+5 bludgeoning. Builds Frostbite on frost hits. 2–3 legendary actions (mace jab; frost-step reposition; in P2, a frost pulse). Phase trigger: at 50% HP he frenzies (Phase 2).

Moveset — Phase 1

1 · Overhead Mace Smash (the clean teaching swing). W1 he hauls the mace up over his shoulder, frost steaming · W2 it screams down in an overhead arc · W3 it craters the stone. Target: one PC, 2d8+5.

W1W2W3
Dodge—ADVDIS
Blockchipchipchip
ParryDISDISADV

Tags: parryable on impact (W3); blockable, but a heavy mace only chips through (no stagger).

2 · Wide Sweep. W1 winds the mace back across his body · W2 a horizontal sweep (10-ft arc) · W3 over-rotation. Target: arc, 2d8+5 + Frostbite.

W1W2W3
Dodge—ADV—
Blockhalfhalfhalf
ParryXXX

Tags: unparryable; dodge under the swing on W2.

3 · Frost Charge (cluster punisher). W1 he lowers his head/mantle and paws the ground · W2 he barrels forward in a line · W3 skids to a stop. Target: all in a line, 3d8+5 + heavy Frostbite + knocked prone.

W1W2W3
DodgeADV (sidestep the lane)X—
BlockXXX
ParryXXX

Tags: undodgeable head-on (it tracks the lane) — sidestep OUT of the lane on W1. Unblockable (a charge) and unparryable.

Moveset — Phase 2 (frenzy at 50% HP)

He moves faster, gains a cold aura (any creature that ends its turn within 10 ft gains 1 Frostbite), spams the Frost Charge, and adds:

4 · Frostbreath Cone. W1 he inhales, mantle crackling with rime · W2 a 20-ft cone of freezing breath · W3 the air hangs white. Target: cone, 3d8 cold + 2 Frostbite.

W1W2W3
DodgeADV (clear the cone)DIS—
BlockXXX
ParryXXX

Tags: unblockable, unparryable; dodge early to leave the cone.

5 · Grab-Leap. W1 he coils and fixes on one PC (clear tell) · W2 he leaps and seizes them · W3 slams them down. Target: one PC, 3d10 + prone + 2 Frostbite.

W1W2W3
DodgeADVDISX
BlockXXX
ParryXXX

Tags: unblockable, unparryable; dodge on the read (W1).

How the exam tests them

  • Spread out or the Charge and Cone hit the whole party.
  • Manage Frostbite — clustering near him in P2 stacks the aura into Exhaustion and a cascade.
  • Bait the charge, then punish the W3 skid.
  • Bring fire/thunder — he is vulnerable; a pyromancer or Lightning Spear shortens P2 dramatically.

Tell Ladder — Vordt

  1. Wipe 1: "The cold builds on you with every frost hit — when the bar fills you start to freeze. Don't huddle near him." (Reveals Frostbite/aura.)
  2. Wipe 2: "His charge follows the one it picks — break sideways out of the lane as he sets his feet; then his big overhead leaves him open right as it lands." (Sidestep Charge W1; parry Overhead W3.)
  3. Wipe 3: "His frozen joints crack under fire and lightning — hit him with flame and he buckles." (Confirms the fire/thunder vulnerability.)

Every regular enemy has a single signature move with its own small Defensive Profile, so it doubles as practice for the boss movesets and resolves fast. Stat bands are tuned for a level 5–7 party of 3–6. Use the mook default DCs (Dodge 10 / Parry 12) unless noted.

Hollow (crawling)

AC 10 · HP 7 · Souls 30. Move — Desperate Lunge: W1 it gathers · W2 it flails forward · W3 it sprawls. 1d6+1. Dodge ADV on W2; parryable W2 (DIS elsewhere). Trivial — teaches the rhythm.

Hollow Soldier (sword & shield)

AC 14 · HP 16 · Souls 50. Move — Shielded Chop: W1 raises sword behind shield · W2 chops · W3 recovers behind guard. 1d8+2. Dodge ADV W2; parry ADV W3 (a clean swing). The baseline parry trainer.

Hollow Archer / Assassin

AC 13 · HP 12 · Souls 60. Move — Snap Shot / Backstab: W1 draws/positions · W2 looses or strikes from concealment · W3 nocks again. 1d8+3 (advantage if you're unaware). Dodge ADV W2 if you can see the tell; unparryable (ranged). Prioritize closing distance.

Hollow Manservant (elite — courtyard farm spawn)

AC 13 · HP 22 · Souls 100. Move — Heavy Cleaver Overhead: W1 hefts the cleaver high · W2 brings it down · W3 buries it, slow to pull free. 2d6+3. Dodge ADV W2; parry ADV W3; blockable, but the heavy cleaver only chips through (no stagger). The reliable farm target.

Lothric Knight (elite guard)

AC 17 · HP 30 · Souls 150. Move — Disciplined Thrust: W1 sets stance · W2 precise lunge (reach) · W3 returns to guard. 1d10+4. Dodge ADV (sidestep) W2; parry ADV W2 (clean thrust); blocking is solid but he punishes whiffs. A real threat in numbers.

Starving Hound (fast pack)

AC 13 · HP 9 · Souls 40. Move — Pounce: W1 crouches · W2 leaps · W3 lands snapping. 1d6+2 + knockdown on a fail. Dodge ADV W2; unparryable. Dangerous in a pack — they swarm and knock prone.

Dragon-corpse Hollow (Pus seeds)

AC 12 · HP 14 · Souls 50 · Vulnerable: fire. Move — Twitching Grab: W1 a slow reach · W2 it clutches · W3 clings. 1d6+2 + grab. Dodge ADV on the early read (W1); unblockable. These are the corrupted hollows near where the Pus of Man erupts — fire clears them fast.

Using these in the farm pocket

The Hollow Courtyard mixes Soldiers, Manservants, and Hounds. They respawn on every bonfire rest, so a struggling party can grind souls here: a full clear is roughly 400–600 souls. Don't let farming drag — two or three clears should close any level gap before Vordt.
